+# AI Dispatch Log
+
+- Timestamp: 2026-05-26T03:37:09.4492087+03:00

+- Issue URL: https://github.com/RustCADs/RGE/issues/200
+- Branch: $Branch
+- Loop exit code: $LoopExit
+- Codex control verdict: $Verdict
+- Loop log: $LoopLog
+
+## Process Trace
+
+1. Queue selected the oldest open ai-dispatch issue.
+2. Queue labelled the issue ai-dispatch-running.
+3. Queue created branch ai-dispatch/ISSUE-200.
+4. Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1 ran Codex plan, Claude gate, Claude execute, and Codex control.
+5. Queue wrote this detailed log before staging, committing, merging, or pushing.
+6. If and only if exit code is 0 and Codex control verdict is pass, queue will fast-forward main and push origin/main.
+

+

Orchestrator protocol does not require REVIEW/CLOSEOUT packets for this dispatch shape.","exit_code":0}],"commit_readiness":"ready_for_publish","commands_run":["git status --short --branch","Get-Content -Raw AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md","Get-Content -Raw ai_handoffs/AI_HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md","Get-Content -Raw ai_handoffs/ISSUE-200_TASK_2026-05-26_03-18-59+0300.md","Get-Content -Raw ai_handoffs/ISSUE-200_EXEC_2026-05-26_03-29-37+0300.md","Get-Content -Raw .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-200/verification.round0.log","git diff","git diff --check","git status --short --untracked-files=all","Get-Content -Raw ai_handoffs/ISSUE-200_EXEC_2026-05-26_03-29-37+0300.meta.json","Get-ChildItem -Name .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-200","findstr /n source-inspection patterns in Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1 Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1 Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1","Get-Content .ai/dispatch-trace/*.jsonl for recent Auto/Queue traces","Get-Content -Raw .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-182/codex.control.round0.json","Get-Content -Raw .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-184/codex.control.round0.json","Get-Content -Raw .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-186/codex.control.round0.json","Get-Content -Raw .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-196/codex.control.round0.json","Get-Content -Raw .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-196/codex.control.round1.json","Get-Content -Raw .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-198/codex.control.round0.json","git show -s --format=%B 94c1254","git log -1 --oneline","git diff --cached --name-status","git status --porcelain=v1 --untracked-files=all"]}

+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-200
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-26_03-29-37+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-200_TASK_2026-05-26_03-18-59+0300.md — TASK packet driving this audit.
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-200_EXEC_2026-05-26_03-29-37+0300.md — this report.
+- Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1 — inspected (read-only) for Auto-layer wiring, recovery, traces.
+- Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1 — inspected (read-only) for queue scope guard, taxonomy, traces.
+- Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1 — inspected (read-only) for watchdog, retries, snapshot, preflight.
+- .ai/dispatch-trace/*.jsonl — Auto and Queue timing traces for 2026-05-25T23:05Z through 2026-05-26T03:18Z.
+- .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-{182,184,186,188,190,192,194,196,198,200}/ — recent run directories incl. control round JSON.
+- ai_dispatch_logs/log_2026-05-26_02-41-41+0300.md — most recent queue log (ISSUE-198).
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-198_TASK_2026-05-26_02-14-41+0300.md — TASK packet referenced as scope-guard-block evidence.
+- ai_handoffs/templates/TASK_PACKET.md — template grammar referenced as planner-prompt source-of-truth.
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-200_TASK_2026-05-26_03-18-59+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- No source edits. This is a read-only automation safety and throughput audit.
+
+### Tests
+
+- None added. The Task Packet's `### MAY edit` and `### MAY add new files` lists explicitly forbid tests, fixtures, or non-handoff artifacts.
+
+### Docs
+
+- None added. The Task Packet forbids docs/ADRs/status edits.
+
+## Audit Findings
+
+### Q1. Feature inventory (current code)
+
+Each named feature is present in the active codebase, scoped to its intended file(s), and remains bounded / opt-in where the original landing dispatch intended:
+
+- **Codex stall watchdog** — implemented in `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` (`Invoke-NativeWithLog`, lines 437–565; arms only after `OutFile` is non-empty; default threshold `300` via `-CodexStallThresholdSec`, range `[0, 3600]`). Wired into `Invoke-CodexExec` at line 587 with the same threshold and the failure-path wording `codex exec stalled: no log growth for …s after first output` (line 589). Disabled when `CodexStallThresholdSec=0`. Stalled returns `{ Code=125; TimedOut=$true; Stalled=$true }`.
+- **Opt-in preflight audit** — `Invoke-CodexPreflightAudit` (`Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` lines 599–745) is gated by `[switch]$EnablePreflightAudit` (param line 94, gate site line 1438). Writes `.ai/dispatch-<id>/codex.preflight.md` only after marker-delimited body validation succeeds; otherwise `Fail` is called and no stale checklist is reused. **Opt-in confirmed.** Activation gap noted in Q4.
+- **Queue scope guard** — `Invoke-QueueScopeGuard` (`Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` lines 600–656) runs at line 1290, BEFORE `git add -A` (line 1294), `git commit`, `git checkout main`, merge, or push. Fails closed when (a) the active TASK packet cannot be located, (b) it declares no positive allowed-path tokens, or (c) any changed/untracked path is outside the union of dispatch-artifact rules, the exact queue-log path, and TASK-derived positive tokens. Allowed-tokens parser at `Get-TaskPositiveAllowedTokens` lines 451–487 — quarantines `ai_handoffs/` and `ai_dispatch_logs/` tokens, skips fenced code blocks, and only accepts **backtick-quoted** path tokens (regex `'\`([^\`]+)\`'` at line 477).
+- **JSONL trace persistence** — Auto: `Initialize-TimingTraceJsonl` + `Write-TimingTrace` (`Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` lines 90–149). Queue: identical pattern (`Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` lines 72–141). Both write `.ai/dispatch-trace/<leaf>-<stamp>-<pid>.jsonl` only when `$TraceTimingEnabled` (param or `RGE_AI_DISPATCH_TRACE_TIMING` env). Writes are best-effort, never throw, never affect exit code. Files present and growing: see `.ai/dispatch-trace/Invoke-AiDispatchAuto-2026052[5-6]-*.jsonl` (7 Auto, 7 Queue files at audit time). **Loop has no `-TraceTiming` param** — see Q4.
+- **Empty-tick speedup** — `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`: per ISSUE-188 EXEC report (`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-188_EXEC_2026-05-25_23-12-35+0300.md`, lines 23–37) the prior two-poll, five-second `Start-Sleep` empty-queue retry loop was deleted; an empty primary `gh issue list` now falls through immediately to `Get-OpenQueueIssuesRest`. Confirmed in the most recent Auto trace `.ai/dispatch-trace/Invoke-AiDispatchAuto-20260526-031812-*.jsonl`: `auto.queue-check primary done (count=0)` at elapsed 2.53s; `auto.cap-check start` at 3.10s — a ≈0.57s transition with no 10-second sleep.
+- **Failure taxonomy labels** — `Get-FailureTaxonomyLabels` (`Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` lines 1006–1047): pure text classifier, never empty, applied at line 1458. Ordering: `publish` > `blocked` > `stall` > `timeout` > `verification` > `control` > `unknown`. Watchdog stall wording is never demoted to a generic timeout.
+- **Read-only same-phase retry** — `Invoke-WithSamePhaseRetry` (`Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` lines 114–165) wraps **only** Claude plan gate (line 1410) and Codex control (line 1533). `RetryCount` default 1; on exhaustion the original `Fail` wording (including watchdog stall text) is re-surfaced; on a per-attempt failure the gate's `.md` / `.envelope.json` / `.stderr.txt` (and the control's `.json` / `.log`) artifacts are removed before the next attempt so a partial first attempt cannot be mistaken for the retry result. `RetryableFailEnabled` is reset to `$false` in `finally` so unrelated `Fail` callers still exit the process.
+- **One-shot transient recovery** — `Get-RecoveryDecision` (`Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` lines 255–336): bounded to exactly one open `ai-dispatch-failed` autonomous issue, taxonomy ∈ `{ai-dispatch-failure-stall, ai-dispatch-failure-timeout}`, no `ai-dispatch-recovered-transient` marker. Successful recovery removes `failed`, adds `queue` + `retry` + `recover` (marker), keeps the transient label as audit evidence. `RecoveryDrainSeeded` flag prevents the same tick from filing new work after a recovered relabel (per the ISSUE-196 round-0 finding referenced in the comment at line 572).
+- **Snapshot-backed mutation retry** — `New-MutationSnapshot` + `Restore-MutationSnapshot` + `Remove-MutationSnapshot` + `Invoke-WithMutationRetry` (`Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` lines 167–344). Snapshot uses `git stash create` + a `refs/dispatch-snapshot/<label>` ref to pin the stash commit, plus an in-memory byte map of untracked non-ignored files. Restore runs `git reset --hard HEAD` → `git clean -fd` → `git stash apply --index` → rewrite untracked manifest. Wraps `Invoke-ClaudeExecute` at line 1457 and both `Invoke-CorrectionPacket` call sites at lines 1514, 1557. `RetryCount=0` short-circuits to single-attempt behavior; the EXEC_STATUS semantic check (`if ($execResult.status -ne 'executed')`, line 1466) is **outside** the wrapper so `blocked`/`failed` verdicts remain terminal and are never retried (the comment at lines 1452–1456 makes this invariant explicit).
+
+### Q2. Safety invariants
+
+Inspected the interactions between queue scope guard, retry paths, transient recovery, branch/publish flow, and snapshot restore. **No path was found where automation could stage or publish work outside the active TASK surface, retry a semantic failure, auto-recover a non-transient failure, or lose user work.**
+
+Specific invariants observed:
+
+- **No publish outside TASK surface.** `Invoke-QueueScopeGuard` runs at `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1:1290` immediately before `git add -A` at line 1294 and well before `git checkout main` / merge / push. Failure paths call `Fail` (line 614/620/648), which exits non-zero before any mutation. Disallowed-path output enumerates every offending entry (line 644–654).
+- **No retry of semantic failures.** `Invoke-WithMutationRetry` wraps only the `Invoke-ClaudeExecute` invocation, not the subsequent `if ($execResult.status -ne 'executed') { Fail … }` block (`Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1:1466`). Similarly, verification-failure handling, Codex control verdict dispatch, and `MaxCorrectionRounds` exhaustion are outside any retry wrapper.
+- **No auto-recovery of non-transient failures.** `Get-RecoveryDecision` (`Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1:255`) requires exactly one open failed autonomous issue and exactly one taxonomy label from `$transientLabels` (line 510 — only `stall` and `timeout`). Multiple failed issues, mixed taxonomy, missing taxonomy, non-transient taxonomy, closed failures, or an existing `ai-dispatch-recovered-transient` marker all fall through to the human-review halt (line 313–322).
+- **No user-work loss in mutation retry.** `New-MutationSnapshot` captures tracked staged+unstaged via `git stash create`, untracked non-ignored as in-memory bytes, and pins the stash with `refs/dispatch-snapshot/<label>` so a stray `git gc` cannot reap the snapshot during the retry window (comments at lines 169–173). `Restore-MutationSnapshot` resets, cleans, replays the stash with `--index`, and re-writes the untracked manifest. The pinned ref is dropped in the `finally` of `Invoke-WithMutationRetry` (lines 340–343).
+- **Stall watchdog can never become a generic timeout label.** `Get-FailureTaxonomyLabels` checks `codex exec stalled` / `no log growth` (line 1031) before checking `timed out` / `timeout` (line 1034). The ordering comment at line 1020 makes this guarantee explicit.
+- **Same-phase retry leaves no partial-attempt artifact masquerading as the retry result.** `CleanupPaths` for plan-gate retry covers `.md` / `.envelope.json` / `.stderr.txt` (line 1412); for control retry covers `.json` / `.log` (line 1535). On exhaustion the original `Fail` wording is re-issued so terminal taxonomy classification is preserved.
+
+**One operational hazard noted but not unsafe**: the scope guard's positive-token parser at `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1:477` requires **backtick-quoted** path tokens. When the Codex Planner emits an `### MAY edit` list with bare bullets (e.g., `- Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` instead of `- \`Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1\``) the parser yields zero positive tokens, the guard fails closed at line 619, and a Codex-control-passing dispatch is blocked from publish. This is *correct* fail-closed behavior — no unsafe work is published — but it is the dominant observed throughput hit (see Q3, Q5).
+
+### Q3. Throughput and trace evidence
+
+Data from `.ai/dispatch-trace/*.jsonl` (Auto and Queue, 2026-05-25T23:05Z → 2026-05-26T03:18Z) plus the `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-{182..198}/codex.control.round*.json` artifacts.
+
+**queue.loop wall-clock per dispatch** (queue.loop start → queue.loop done, exit codes all 0):
+
+| Dispatch | queue.loop (s) | Approx (min) | Control rounds | Notes |
+|---|---|---|---|---|
+| ISSUE-188 | 724 | 12.1 | 1 (pass) | empty-tick speedup landing |
+| ISSUE-190 | 1027 | 17.1 | 1 (pass) | trends CLI |
+| ISSUE-192 | 1040 | 17.3 | 1 (pass) | (queue trace) |
+| ISSUE-194 | 961 | 16.0 | 1 (pass) | JSONL trace trends |
+| ISSUE-196 | 2125 | 35.4 | 2 (needs_changes → pass) | transient recovery landing, slowest run |
+| ISSUE-198 | 1642 | 27.4 | 1 (pass) | scope-guard publish blocker (see below) |
+
+**Median queue.loop ≈ 17 min; max 35 min (ISSUE-196, one correction round).** Range of post-loop overhead (dispatch-log → relabel done) per Queue trace, including the GitHub comment and relabel round-trips: ≈2.7 s (ISSUE-188), 3.1 s (ISSUE-190), 3.2 s (ISSUE-192), 3.6 s (ISSUE-194), 3.6 s (ISSUE-196). Post-loop overhead is dominated by GitHub round-trips (comment ≈1.0–1.1 s, relabel ≈1.5–1.9 s) and is **not** the bottleneck.
+
+**Auto-tick non-queue overhead** (`Invoke-AiDispatchAuto-20260526-031812-*.jsonl`, most recent tick): halt-checks 1.13 s; recovery-check 0.60 s; queue-check primary 0.69 s; cap-check 1.22 s; codex select 15.18 s; gh issue create 1.42 s; ≈29 s total before queue-invocation start. Empty-tick speedup is in effect: `queue-check primary done (count=0)` → `cap-check start` transition is 0.57 s (no 10 s sleep). The earlier ISSUE-198-driving tick `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto-20260526-021300-*.jsonl` showed the same shape with a 65.9 s `codex.select` (heavier brief read) but identical sub-1 s empty-tick fall-through.
+
+**Correction rounds**: 1 of 9 recent dispatches (ISSUE-196) needed a second round (round 0 `needs_changes` → round 1 `pass`). All others were single-round.
+
+**Stalls**: zero `Stalled=true` events in the JSONL traces or queue logs since the watchdog landed. The `ai-dispatch-failure-stall` taxonomy never fired.
+
+**Timeouts**: zero `TimedOut=true` events. No `ai-dispatch-failure-timeout` taxonomy in the recent sequence.
+
+**One scope-guard publish block.** `.ai/dispatch-trace/Invoke-AiDispatchQueue-20260526-021433-*.jsonl` (ISSUE-198) terminates at `queue.guard: scope-check start` with no `scope-check done` event — meaning the guard called `Fail` and the script exited 1. The Auto trace for the same tick records `auto.tick: queue-invocation done (exit=1)` and a halt-sentinel write would have followed had the run not been recovered manually. Confirmed in the published commit message (`git show 94c1254`):
+
+> Manual publish recovery: the queue scope guard rejected the uncommitted branch because the generated TASK packet listed Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1 without the backticked path token grammar the guard requires. The TASK artifact was repaired to \`Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1\`; implementation diff and Codex control verdict were unchanged.
+
+Codex control had already returned `pass` (`.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-198/codex.control.round0.json`: `verdict=pass`, `commit_readiness=ready_for_publish`), verification passed (round 0), and EXEC_STATUS was `executed`. Only the planner's bare-bullet `### MAY edit` formatting tripped the guard.
+
+**Current bottleneck (from data, not intuition):** the dominant *unrecovered* throughput hit in the recent sequence is the queue-scope-guard ↔ Codex-Planner grammar mismatch. queue.loop wall-clock (model latency + verification gate) is also large (17–35 min) but is expected and not regressed — it was the same shape before the hardening chain landed. The scope-guard mismatch caused 1 of the last 9 dispatches to require manual human publish recovery, fully consuming a Codex-pass run.
+
+### Q4. Activation gaps
+
+| Capability | Defined in | Wired by Auto into Queue | Wired by Queue into Loop | Status |
+|---|---|---|---|---|
+| `-EnablePreflightAudit` | `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1:94` | No (Auto param block lines 62–80 omits it; queue invocation line 893–896 omits it) | No (Queue param block lines 44–62 omits it; loop invocation line 1257–1260 omits it) | **Unwired** |
+| `-TraceTiming` | `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1:79`, `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1:61` | **Yes** (`Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1:896` adds `-TraceTiming` to `$queueArgs` when set) | No (Loop has no such parameter; `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1:1257–1260` invokes Loop with only `-MaxPlanRevisions` / `-MaxCorrectionRounds`) | Auto→Queue active; Queue→Loop **unwired** (Loop is silent on the timing channel) |
+| `-MutationRetryCount` | `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1:65` (default 1) | n/a | Not passed; Loop default 1 takes effect | Active via default |
+| `-CodexStallThresholdSec` | `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1:89` (default 300) | n/a | Not passed; Loop default 300 takes effect | Active via default |
+| `-ModelTimeoutSec` | `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1:83` (default 1800) | n/a | Not passed; Loop default 1800 takes effect | Active via default |
+| `-VerifyTimeoutSec` | `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1:86` (default 3600) | n/a | Not passed; Loop default 3600 takes effect | Active via default |
+| `-ClaudePermissionMode`, `-CodexModel`, `-ClaudeModel`, `-AllowDirtyTracked`, `-VerifyScript`, `-SkipVerification` | `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1:67–80` | n/a | Not passed | Loop defaults take effect (intentional for the queue path) |
+
+**`-EnablePreflightAudit`: not wired through Auto and Queue into Loop.** The opt-in pitfall checklist (`Invoke-CodexPreflightAudit`, lines 599–745, wired at the gate site `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1:1438`) currently has no path to be activated by an autonomous Auto-driven dispatch. A human can pass it only when invoking `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` directly.
+
+**`-TraceTiming`: partially wired.** Auto → Queue is correctly forwarded (line 896). Queue → Loop is not wired because Loop has no `-TraceTiming` parameter, so Loop emits no timing trace lines — `queue.loop start … queue.loop done` is the only Loop-window observability the JSONL stream offers. Loop-internal phases (codex plan, claude plan-gate, claude execute, verification, codex control) are visible only through stdout log lines, not as structured JSONL events.
+
+**Other landed-but-not-activated capabilities discovered from current code**: no other dispatch-relevant switches were found that should be activated but aren't. The remaining Loop parameters in the table above are intentional knobs that have working defaults for the queue path.
+
+### Q5. Smallest safe follow-up
+
+**Title:** Enforce backtick-quoted path-token grammar in the Codex Planner prompt so the queue scope guard's positive-token parser accepts the generated `### MAY edit` / `### MAY add new files` entries.
+
+**Rationale (from Q1–Q4):** The scope guard at `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1:477` only recognizes backtick-quoted path tokens (`'\`([^\`]+)\`'`). When the Codex Planner emits an `### MAY edit` list as bare Markdown bullets, the guard returns zero positive tokens and fails closed — blocking publish for a Codex-control-passing, verification-passing, EXEC-status-executed dispatch (observed: ISSUE-198, requiring manual TASK repair to publish via PR #199, commit `94c1254`). The Planner prompt in `Invoke-PlanFill` (`Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1:926–954`) currently has no instruction requiring backtick-wrapping; it only says "Make scope precise: MAY edit, MUST NOT edit, deliverables, gates, halt conditions." This is the smallest single-file change that prevents the recurrence without touching the scope guard, the template grammar, or any other landed capability.
+
+**Allowed files** (the smallest set sufficient to fix the planner output for new dispatches):
+
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` — add to the Rules list in the `Invoke-PlanFill` prompt (lines 943–953) an explicit instruction that every path or glob token inside `### MAY edit` and `### MAY add new files` MUST be wrapped in backticks (with a one-line worked example), AND that bare-bulleted paths are not accepted by the queue scope guard.
+- Dispatch's own generated `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-<n>_EXEC_*.md` packet (and `.meta.json` sidecar) under `ai_handoffs/`.
+- Dispatch's own `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*ISSUE-<n>*.md` if produced by the queue runner.
+
+**Must-not-touch surfaces:**
+
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`, `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`, `Register-AiDispatchSchedule.ps1`, `Wait-GitHubActions.ps1`, `Get-AiDispatchHealth.ps1`, `Get-AiDispatchTrends.ps1`, `Watch-AiDispatch.ps1`.
+- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`, `.ai/codex_control.schema.json`, `.ai/handoff.schema.json`, `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`.
+- `Invoke-QueueScopeGuard` / `Get-TaskPositiveAllowedTokens` / the regex at line 477 — the parser is correct as-is; do not relax it.
+- `ai_handoffs/templates/TASK_PACKET.md` — keep the template change out of this dispatch; addressing it is a separate scope decision.
+- `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md`, `AI_DISPATCH_PARALLEL.md`, `ai_handoffs/AI_HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md`, all schemas, the failure-taxonomy text, the retry / recovery / publish policies, any Cargo/workflow/scheduler file, any Rust source or test.
+- All other dispatches' handoff packets or sidecars.
+
+**Verification gates** (must pass and be documented in the follow-up EXEC report):
+
+- PowerShell parser validation of `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` via `[System.Management.Automation.Language.Parser]::ParseFile(...)` — expected: `PARSE OK`, zero parser errors.
+- `git diff --check` from repo root — expected exit 0.
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=all` — expected: only the follow-up dispatch's own handoff/log artifacts present; `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` modified.
+- `.\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1` — expected: VERIFY OK across all canonical steps (the script is unchanged but the run validates that the planner-prompt edit does not regress workspace gates).
+- Static inspection confirming the diff is limited to the Rules list inside the `Invoke-PlanFill` here-string (no behavior change outside that prompt string).
+
+**Halt conditions** (the Executor MUST set `HANDOFF_STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN` if any of these occur):
+
+- The change requires editing `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`, `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`, the scope-guard parser, the schemas, or the TASK_PACKET template.
+- The Codex Planner does not honor a prompt-only rule after the edit lands (i.e., the next autonomous dispatch still emits bare-bulleted `### MAY edit`). That would escalate the fix from a prompt rule to a template grammar change or a Planner-output linter, requiring policy arbitration.
+- The change risks invalidating the queue scope guard's existing safety invariants (e.g., by adding a section heading or grammar shift the parser would treat as a positive token).
+- The verification gate or any pre-existing test fails as a result of the prompt-string edit.
+- The repository contains unrelated tracked changes that would prevent proving the edit is scoped to one file.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+No files modified. All evidence cited above was gathered via read-only inspection (`Read`, `Grep`, `git log`, `git status --short`, `git show`, and structured reads of `.jsonl` traces and `.json` control artifacts).
+
+## Verification Results
+
+- `git diff --check` — exit 0 (no whitespace or conflict markers).
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=all` — final output (after EXEC packet scaffold):
+  ```
+  ?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-200_EXEC_2026-05-26_03-29-37+0300.md
+  ?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-200_TASK_2026-05-26_03-18-59+0300.md
+  ?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-200_TASK_2026-05-26_03-18-59+0300.meta.json
+  ```
+  No tracked source, test, doc, Cargo, workflow, schema, task-brief, or automation-script changes. Untracked items are limited to this dispatch's own TASK packet + sidecar and this EXEC packet. The EXEC `.meta.json` sidecar will be produced by `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize`. No `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-*/automation-audit-scratch.md` was created; the token in the TASK packet was advisory only.
+- Static read-only inspection: no production file (source/test/doc/Cargo/workflow/schema/task brief/automation script) was edited by this audit.
+- EXEC-report completeness check: Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4, Q5 are each present above. Q1 cites file/line evidence for each of the nine named features. Q2 lists invariants with file:line references. Q3 cites JSONL trace files, control-round JSON artifacts, and the `git show 94c1254` commit message. Q4 enumerates the wiring of `EnablePreflightAudit` and `TraceTiming` plus the other Loop params. Q5 names exactly one smallest safe follow-up with title, allowed files, must-not-touch surfaces, verification gates, and halt conditions.
+
+## Deviations from Task Packet
+
+None — execution stayed strictly within the Task Packet scope. No tracked file outside this dispatch's own handoff artifacts was created or modified. No optional `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-*/automation-audit-scratch.md` was needed.
+
+## Open Questions for Reviewer
+
+- Q5 deliberately picks the Planner-prompt edit over a TASK_PACKET template edit and over relaxing the scope guard, because the prompt edit is the smallest single-file change at the exact point where the bare-bullet output originates. If policy prefers the template edit (or both), please route a follow-up TASK packet with that scope rather than expanding ISSUE-200's correction round.
+- Loop-side `-TraceTiming` (Queue → Loop wiring) is *not* the recommended Q5 follow-up because no stall or timeout has fired in the recent sequence and the trace gap is observability-only, not blocking. The scope-guard / planner-prompt mismatch IS blocking (1 of 9 recent runs), so it wins the "smallest safe follow-up justified by current evidence" test.
+
+## Worktree State
+
+- Tracked files: clean — no tracked source, test, doc, Cargo, workflow, schema, task brief, or automation script changes.
+- Untracked items:
+  - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-200_TASK_2026-05-26_03-18-59+0300.md` (TASK packet)

+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-200
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-26_03-18-59+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md

+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Perform GitHub issue #200 as a read-only automation safety and throughput audit after the retry sequence. The audit must inspect the currently landed automation implementation and current dispatch evidence, answer the required audit questions, and name exactly one smallest safe follow-up with its allowed files, verification gates, and halt conditions, or return `NEEDS_HUMAN` if the evidence does not justify one bounded follow-up.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- None. This is an audit-only dispatch. The Executor must not edit tracked source, tests, docs, Cargo files, workflows, schemas, task briefs, automation scripts, or existing handoff/log artifacts.
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`
+- `Get-AiDispatchTrends.ps1`
+- `Get-AiDispatchHealth.ps1`
+- `Watch-AiDispatch.ps1`
+- `Register-AiDispatchSchedule.ps1`
+- `Wait-GitHubActions.ps1`
+- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`
+- `.ai/codex_control.schema.json`
+- `.ai/handoff.schema.json`
+- `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`
+- `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md`
+- `AI_DISPATCH_PARALLEL.md`
+- `ai_handoffs/AI_HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md`
+- `ai_handoffs/templates/`
+- `Cargo.toml`
+- `Cargo.lock`
+- `.github/workflows/`
+- Any Rust source or test file under `crates/**`, `kernel/**`, `editor/**`, or `runtime/**`
+- Any docs, ADRs, status files, schemas, JSONL trace files, task brief/backlog content, CI/workflow files, scheduler artifacts, `.gitignore`, existing `ai_dispatch_logs/**` artifacts, or handoff packet for another dispatch
+- This TASK packet after execution begins
+
+### MAY add new files
+- This dispatch's own generated EXEC packet matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-200_EXEC_*.md`
+- This dispatch's own generated CORRECT packet matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-200_CORRECT_*.md`, only if the orchestrator creates a correction round
+- Matching `.meta.json` sidecars for this dispatch's own TASK, EXEC, or CORRECT packets when packet finalization creates them
+- This dispatch's own queue log matching `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*ISSUE-200*.md`, if produced by the queue runner
+- Optional ignored run-dir scratch file `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-*/automation-audit-scratch.md` for temporary local notes only. The Executor does not need to create this file; the token exists to keep read-only audit tasks compatible with the current fail-closed queue scope guard.
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+- New source files, tests, docs, ADRs, schemas, JSONL trace files, workflow files, scheduler files, Cargo files, dashboards, scripts, generated assets, fixtures, task-brief entries, GitHub automation files, or persistent analysis artifacts
+- New handoff packets or sidecars for any dispatch other than ISSUE-200
+- New `ai_dispatch_logs/**` files by hand
+- Any tracked file outside this dispatch's own handoff/log artifacts
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- Produce one `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-200_EXEC_*.md` report that explicitly answers Q1 through Q5 below.
+- Cite the exact local evidence consulted for each answer: script/function names or line references where practical, JSONL trace files, `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-*` run artifacts, queue logs, and recent handoff packets.
+- Confirm whether `-EnablePreflightAudit` is currently wired from `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` through `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` to `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`.
+- Confirm whether `-TraceTiming` is currently wired from Auto into Queue, and note any other landed-but-not-activated automation capability discovered from the current code.
+- Summarize bottlenecks, correction rounds, stalls, and timeouts from current `.ai/dispatch-trace/*.jsonl` data plus recent dispatch artifacts for issues #182 through #198.
+- Identify exactly one smallest safe implementation follow-up, including title, allowed files, must-not-touch surfaces, verification gates, and halt conditions, or return `NEEDS_HUMAN` with the specific decision required.
+- Document `git diff --check` and final `git status --short --untracked-files=all`, showing no tracked source, test, doc, Cargo, workflow, schema, task brief, or automation script changes.
+
+## Audit Questions
+
+The EXEC report must answer these questions explicitly, using the labels Q1 through Q5:
+
+- Q1. Feature inventory: confirm from current code whether each recently landed automation feature is present, scoped to its intended files, and still opt-in or bounded where specified: Codex stall watchdog, opt-in preflight audit, queue scope guard, JSONL trace persistence, empty-tick speedup, failure taxonomy labels, read-only same-phase retry, one-shot transient recovery, and snapshot-backed mutation retry.
+- Q2. Safety invariants: inspect the interaction between queue scope guard, retry paths, transient recovery, branch/publish flow, and snapshot restore. Identify any path where automation could stage or publish work outside the active TASK surface, retry a semantic failure, auto-recover a non-transient failure, or lose user work. If none are found, say so explicitly.
+- Q3. Throughput and trace evidence: use current `.ai/dispatch-trace/*.jsonl` plus recent dispatch artifacts for issues #182 through #198 to summarize observed wall-clock, queue-loop, empty-cap, GitHub finalize, correction, stall, and timeout behavior. Name the current bottleneck based on data, not intuition.
+- Q4. Activation gaps: verify whether `-EnablePreflightAudit` is currently wired through Auto and Queue into Loop. Verify whether `-TraceTiming` is wired through Auto into Queue. Name any other landed-but-not-activated automation capability discovered from current code.
+- Q5. Smallest safe follow-up: name exactly one smallest implementation dispatch only if it is justified by Q1 through Q4. Include title, allowed files, must-not-touch surfaces, verification gates, and halt conditions. If the safest next step needs human arbitration, write `NEEDS_HUMAN` and state the decision required. Do not propose a broad rewrite, dashboard, new agent, product task, or multi-item bundle as Q5.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- The audit is read-only: no tracked file is edited except this dispatch's own generated handoff/log artifacts.
+- The EXEC report inspects current implementations of watchdog, preflight audit, queue scope guard, JSONL trace persistence, empty-tick speedup, failure taxonomy, read-only retry, transient recovery, and mutation retry.
+- The EXEC report answers whether `EnablePreflightAudit` is currently wired through Auto and Queue to Loop.
+- The EXEC report uses current JSONL traces and recent dispatch artifacts to summarize bottlenecks, correction rounds, stalls, and timeouts.
+- The EXEC report identifies exactly one smallest safe follow-up with allowed files, verification gates, and halt conditions, or returns `NEEDS_HUMAN` with the required arbitration decision.
+- The immediate follow-up, if any, is a single bounded implementation dispatch. It is not a broad rewrite, dashboard, new agent, product task, or multi-item bundle.
+- Final worktree state contains only this dispatch's own generated handoff/log artifacts plus optional ignored `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-*/automation-audit-scratch.md`.
+- `git diff --check` exits 0.
+- Final `git status --short --untracked-files=all` is reported and shows no tracked source, test, doc, Cargo, workflow, schema, task brief, or automation script changes.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- Do not implement any code change.
+- Do not edit or add tests.
+- Do not edit documentation, ADRs, status files, schemas, workflows, Cargo files, task briefs, automation scripts, JSONL traces, failure taxonomy labels, queue policy, retry policy, publish policy, scheduler behavior, or GitHub issue/label automation.
+- Do not stage, commit, push, open a PR, edit GitHub labels, post GitHub comments outside normal queue bookkeeping, or mutate remote state.
+- Do not create dashboards, trend products, new agents, new scheduled tasks, new workflow jobs, broad refactors, or multi-task implementation plans.
+- Do not infer dispatch completion from missing `_REVIEW_` or `_CLOSEOUT_` packets. Per `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md`, orchestrator dispatches normally produce TASK and EXEC packets, optional CORRECT packets, and control JSON in `.ai/dispatch-*`.
+- Do not treat the audit as permission to fix any defect found. Defects become Q5 evidence for one smallest follow-up, or `NEEDS_HUMAN`.
+- If live `gh` commands are used at all, they must be read-only inspection commands. Prefer local traces, run directories, queue logs, and handoff packets.
+- Temporary files, if any, must be confined to ignored scratch or system temp and removed before the EXEC report finishes unless they are the optional ignored audit scratch named above.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each gate in the EXEC report:
+
+- `git diff --check` from the repository root. Expected exit 0.
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=all` from the repository root. Expected: no tracked source, test, doc, Cargo, workflow, schema, task brief, or automation script changes; only this dispatch's own handoff/log artifacts and optional ignored `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-*/automation-audit-scratch.md` may be present.
+- Static read-only inspection confirming no production files were edited by the audit.
+- EXEC-report completeness check: Q1 through Q5 are all present, each cites evidence, and Q5 contains exactly one smallest safe follow-up or `NEEDS_HUMAN`.
+
+If the orchestrator later routes a correction because its canonical verification gate fails outside this audit scope, the Executor must not edit source or tests to repair that failure. Instead, write the required EXEC/CORRECT-round report with `STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, `HANDOFF_STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, and `NEXT_ROLE: HUMAN_ARBITER`, naming the out-of-scope gate failure.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt with `HANDOFF_STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN` and `NEXT_ROLE: HUMAN_ARBITER` if any of the following occur:
+
+- Answering Q1 through Q4 would require editing any tracked file outside this dispatch's own generated artifacts.
+- Current code, local JSONL traces, run directories, queue logs, and handoff packets are insufficient to answer Q1 through Q4 without inventing facts.
+- Q5 cannot name exactly one smallest safe follow-up from current evidence.
+- The evidence points to multiple equally necessary immediate follow-ups and the Executor cannot choose one without policy arbitration.
+- The audit would require mutating GitHub labels, comments, issues, branches, or remote state outside normal queue bookkeeping.
+- A verification or correction path asks the Executor to fix source, tests, docs, Cargo, workflows, schemas, task brief, or automation scripts.
+- The worktree contains unrelated tracked changes that make it impossible to prove the audit left source/test/doc/Cargo/script surfaces untouched.
+- The queue scope guard rejects this read-only dispatch even with the allowed generated artifacts and optional ignored scratch token.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+This is the post-sequence audit for the automation-hardening chain. It should read the implementation and evidence, not change it. The important output is a defensible answer to whether the newly landed mechanisms are safe, activated where expected, and improving throughput based on traces. Q5 must be a single smallest next implementation step or `NEEDS_HUMAN`; avoid bundling several reasonable ideas into one follow-up.
+
+Verbatim review-gate strings copied from the issue body:
+
+```text
+MUST perform read-only automation audit only and MUST NOT edit source tests docs Cargo workflows schemas task brief or automation scripts
+MUST inspect current implementations of watchdog preflight scope guard JSONL trace empty-tick speedup taxonomy read-only retry transient recovery and mutation retry
+MUST answer whether EnablePreflightAudit is currently wired through Auto and Queue to Loop
+MUST use current JSONL traces and recent dispatch artifacts to summarize bottlenecks correction rounds stalls and timeouts
+MUST identify exactly one smallest safe follow-up with allowed files verification gates and halt conditions or return NEEDS_HUMAN
+MUST NOT propose broad rewrites dashboards new agents product work or multi-item bundles as the immediate follow-up
+MUST leave only this dispatch's own handoff/log artifacts plus optional ignored .ai dispatch scratch
+MUST run git diff --check and report git status showing no tracked source/test/doc/Cargo/script changes
+```
+
+No prior Claude gate result exists for this revision.
+
+---
+
+HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE
+NEXT_ROLE: EXECUTOR_AI
+EXIT_CODE: 0
+
